headline: FIRST AGENT BASELINE for Palau (rotation backfill, never assessed). Published composite 53.1 / Functional (rank 43) sustained against compound 2024-2026 evidence: (1) March 2024 COFA renewal with US providing $889M / 20-year grant assistance and trust fund contributions; CRA in force; significant climate, education, infrastructure, healthcare allocations; (2) Active marine conservation governance — Palau National Marine Sanctuary one of world's largest; (3) Active climate-vulnerability framing in international fora — Pacific climate-leadership posture, partner to Vanuatu UN climate accountability resolution; (4) US high-frequency radar system completion expected June 2026 — strategic-deployment context introducing INT/BND mixed signal (sovereignty trade-off for security/climate funding); (5) Compact context historically eroded trust in US government over nuclear-testing-legacy non-reparation (regional Pacific concern, not Palau-specific). Dimensional profile (AWR 3, EMP 3, ACT 3.5, EQU 2.5, BND 3.5, ACC 3, SYS 3.5, INT 3) is consistent with functional-band Pacific-island state with active climate-governance posture and meaningful US partnership scaffolding. Math reconstructs cleanly to 53.1. Confirm at 53.1 / Functional with first-baseline rationale; Medium confidence (limited in-window news but stable structural baseline).
key_evidence: March 2024 Compact of Free Association (COFA) renewal: 20-year extension with $889M total grant assistance and trust fund contributions for Palau; environmental, climate, healthcare, education, infrastructure allocations.
Pacific climate-leadership posture: Palau is among the world's largest marine sanctuaries; active in Pacific Islands Forum and UN climate-accountability initiatives.
US strategic deployment context: high-frequency radar system construction in Palau with June 2026 expected completion — sovereignty-trade-off for security/economic-assistance context.

April 2026 (recent supporting context): Vanuatu UN climate-change resolution as Pacific climate-accountability vehicle — Palau aligned with Pacific-bloc climate-justice positioning per Amnesty April 2026 analysis.
COFA migrants in US: under COFA, Palauan citizens have access to live, work, and study in the US — meaningful welfare-and-mobility scaffolding for citizens.
Regional Pacific concerns: nuclear-testing-legacy (RMI-specific, not Palau) erodes broader US-trust signal in Pacific bloc; relevant SYS context not directly applicable to Palau dimensional scoring.
No structural news events in May 8 window that disrupt the published baseline. First agent baseline confirms the structural profile.

evidence.source: US Joint Economic Committee — Renewed Compacts of Free Association Support US Economic, National Security, and Climate Goals
evidence.finding: March 2024 COFA renewal; $7.1B regional economic assistance; $889M Palau-specific allocation over 20 years; climate, environment, infrastructure, healthcare, education emphasis.

evidence.source: Congressional Research Service — Compacts of Free Association
evidence.finding: COFA structural framework; US-Palau bilateral relationship; military strategic context.

evidence.source: Amnesty International April 2026 — Vanuatu UN climate-change resolution
evidence.finding: Pacific-bloc climate-accountability framing; supports Palau alignment with climate-justice positioning.

evidence.source: ReliefWeb — Island nation takes on world's polluters (Palau)
evidence.finding: Palau-specific climate-accountability and marine-conservation posture documented.
Human rights and climate change country profile for Palau; vulnerability documentation.

scoring_rationale.AWR: 3.0 sustained. Active Pacific Islands Forum climate-monitoring participation and marine-conservation governance demonstrate suffering-detection capacity for climate-vulnerability vector. Anchor 3.
scoring_rationale.EMP: 3.0 sustained. International communications emphasise climate-vulnerability and displacement-risk affective acknowledgment; Pacific-bloc solidarity framing. Anchor 3.
scoring_rationale.ACT: 3.5 sustained. COFA-funded climate, environment, healthcare, education programs operational; marine sanctuary protections active; proportionate small-state action capacity. Anchor 3-4 with 3.5 reflecting active climate-adaptation programs.
scoring_rationale.EQU: 2.5 sustained. Wealth concentration not a structural issue at Palau scale but COFA-related Palauan diaspora in US faces specific welfare-access asymmetries; indigenous land-tenure and ocean-rights distribution generally well-managed. Anchor 2-3.
scoring_rationale.BND: 3.5 sustained. US military strategic deployment (radar system) introduces sovereignty trade-off; declared boundaries of US-Palau cooperation are documented and consent-based via COFA renewal. Marine-protected-area scope-of-protection clear. Anchor 3-4 with 3.5.
scoring_rationale.ACC: 3.0 sustained. Government transparency in international fora active; domestic civil society space functional; COFA reporting structures provide bilateral accountability. Anchor 3.
scoring_rationale.SYS: 3.5 sustained. COFA institutional framework provides structural systemic scaffolding; Pacific Islands Forum multilateral participation; UN small-island-developing-state framework engagement. Anchor 3-4 with 3.5.
scoring_rationale.INT: 3.0 sustained. Declared climate-leadership and sovereignty-preservation commitments consistent with COFA renewal terms and marine-sanctuary operational reality. Anchor 3.

math_check.validator_check: Reconstructs cleanly to published 53.1. No math-hygiene flag. Sub-threshold delta. First agent baseline confirms functional-band placement.

negativeSignals: US military strategic deployment (high-frequency radar June 2026 completion) introduces sovereignty trade-off; small-state structural vulnerability to US policy shifts; climate-displacement existential risk for Pacific-island population; regional COFA-bloc historical grievances over nuclear-testing-legacy (RMI) erode broader US-trust signal in Pacific bloc.
positiveSignals: March 2024 COFA renewal with $889M / 20-year package emphasising climate, environment, healthcare, education, infrastructure; Palau marine sanctuary among world's largest; active Pacific Islands Forum climate-leadership; Vanuatu UN climate-resolution alignment; democratic governance functional; COFA-mobility access for Palauan citizens to US live/work/study.
sector_context: Countries — Pacific small-island-developing-state (SIDS) cluster. Cross-reference Samoa (also assessed this cycle), Marshall Islands, Federated States of Micronesia, Vanuatu. Palau distinguished by active marine-conservation governance and stable functional-band placement. No structural events in May 8 window disrupt the baseline. Rotation backfill complete.
open_actions_resolved: First agent baseline established for Palau at 53.1 / Functional
open_actions_carried: Watch: US radar system completion June 2026 and any sovereignty/operational impact
Watch: Vanuatu UN climate-change resolution progress and Palau alignment
Watch: COFA implementation reporting and any disbursement delays
Watch: any climate-displacement event in Palau
